title: Affiliated operators for classical and quantum control
abstract: Using techniques from the theory of von Neumann algebras, we propose a framework for addressing questions of controllability of bilinear systems on infinite dimensional Hilbert spaces. In the setup, we assume only that the drift and control terms arising in a bilinear control system are affiliated with a von Neumann algebra of finite type acting on the same Hilbert space. When the control terms satisfy basic norm bound conditions, we prove existence of time-optimal controls. In the more general setting where all operators may be unbounded, we show how the dynamical Lie algebra for the system is still well-defined and may be used to check approximate controllability of the system in question. We discuss how this approach can be applied to classical dynamical systems through the Koopman operator formalism, and investigate potential candidates for the von Neumann algebra which may guide the choice of controls. We illustrate how an affiliation relation naturally arises in both classical and quantum control systems with a few examples.
summary: This paper proposes a new mathematical framework for controlling infinite-dimensional systems by using the theory of affiliated operators in von Neumann algebras. It successfully handles the mathematical difficulties of unbounded Hamiltonians, allowing for a well-defined Lie algebraic approach to controllability in both quantum and classical contexts.
main_problem: Addressing the mathematical challenges of controllability and time-optimal control for bilinear systems in infinite-dimensional Hilbert spaces where Hamiltonians are unbounded.
main_result: The authors establish a framework using affiliated operators in finite von Neumann algebras to recover a well-defined dynamical Lie algebra and prove the existence of time-optimal controls.
method: Utilizing von Neumann algebra theory, specifically the properties of $\tau$-measurable and affiliated operators, alongside the Koopman operator formalism for classical systems.
model_or_system: The framework applies to infinite-dimensional quantum systems governed by the Schrödinger equation and classical dynamical systems modeled via the Koopman operator formalism.
key_observables: Reachable sets of vector states and the dynamical Lie algebra structure.
important_parameters: The norm bounds of control terms, the type of von Neumann algebra (finite vs. semifinite), and the spectral properties of the drift operator.
main_assumptions: The drift and control terms are assumed to be affiliated with a von Neumann algebra of finite type; the framework specifically addresses the difficulties of unbounded operators.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the operator-algebraic framework for affiliated operators, develops control theory for quantum systems (existence of optimal controls and reachable sets), extends the approach to classical systems via Koopman theory, and provides specific examples like the Jaynes-Cummings model and differential operators on Lie groups.
why_interesting: It provides a rigorous mathematical foundation for controlling infinite-dimensional systems, which is crucial for advanced quantum control tasks in cavity QED and continuous-variable quantum information where unbounded Hamiltonians are standard.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
